paper_title: Isotopic Analysis of Tree Rings near Steelworks

steps.description: the samples (0.060 mg) were loaded into zin capsules
steps.searchable_summary: weigh and capsule sample for combustion

steps.description: combusted at a temperature of 1100°C

steps.description: CO 2 was separated in gas chromatography column in the elemental analyzer (EuroVector)
steps.searchable_summary: separate CO2 from combustion products using gas chromatography

steps.description: The stable carbon isotope compositions of the samples were determined using an Isoprime continuous flow isotope ratio mass spectrometer (GV Instruments, Manchester, UK) at the Mass Spectrometry Laboratory of the Silesian University of Technology.

steps.description: The δ 13 C results are reported in values relative to VPDB

methods.description: Procedure for preparing and measuring δ18O values from samples using pyrolysis and isotope ratio mass spectrometry.

steps.description: the samples (0.095 mg) were loaded into silver capsules.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh and capsule sample for pyrolysis

steps.description: To displace moisture-containing air in the glucose samples, the samples were heated over 24 h in a vacuum line (60°C)
steps.searchable_summary: dry sample under vacuum at controlled temperature

steps.description: after that, they were put into a special air-filled homemade dry box before stable isotope ratio determination (Sensuła et al. 2011b).
steps.searchable_summary: store dried samples in a dry environment

steps.description: The samples were converted to CO by pyrolysis at a temperature of 1350°C
steps.searchable_summary: pyrolyze sample at high temperature to convert to CO

steps.description: separated in gas chromatography column in the elemental analyzer (EuroVector).
steps.searchable_summary: separate CO from pyrolysis products using gas chromatography

steps.description: The stable oxygen ... isotope compositions of the samples were determined using an Isoprime continuous flow isotope ratio mass spectrometer (GV Instruments, Manchester, UK) at the Mass Spectrometry Laboratory of the Silesian University of Technology.

methods.method_name: Combined Carbon and Oxygen Isotope Ratio Analysis
methods.description: Analysis and interpretation of the relationship between δ13C and δ18O values to infer plant physiological responses (stomatal conductance vs. photosynthetic capacity) based on established models.

steps.description: the model (Scheidegger et al. 2000;Farquhar and Lloyd 1993) indicates which factor responded more strongly. Stomatal regulation of water loss concerns plant response to variation in the growth environment.
steps.searchable_summary: apply physiological models relating isotope ratios to plant gas exchange parameters
outputs: inferred relationships or parameters (e.g., Ci/Ca, gs, Amax)

steps.description: The isotopic discrimination in photosynthesis can be described as the model of (Farquhar and Lloyd 1993) where C i is the intercellular CO 2 concentration, C a is the ambient CO 2 concentration, a (ca. 4.4‰) is the discrimination against 13 CO 2 during CO 2 diffusion through stomata, and b (ca. 27‰) is the discrimination associated with carboxylation.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate carbon isotope discrimination from Ci/Ca ratio

steps.description: Positive correlation between δ 13 C and δ 18 O is predicted when Δ 13 C is driven by reduction in stomata conductance and not much changes in A max , whereas the upward trend reflects an increase in A max with no or minor reaction in g s . No relationship between δ 13 C and δ 18 O is expected when variation in Δ 13 C is driven by changes in photosynthetic capacity alone, because δ 18 O is not affected by photosynthetic capacity. If variation in Δ 13 C is driven by increases in both stomata conductance and photosynthetic capacity, then the change in δ 18 O per unit change in Δ 13 C will be greater than if stomata conductance alone had increased (Barbour et al. 2000(Barbour et al. , 2002. These analyses are only qualitative. The analysis of combined measurement of carbon an oxygen isotope ratio (Fig. 4) shows positive correlation between δ 13 C and δ 18 O in two periods of time (before mid-1980s and after mid-1990s).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze the correlation between delta 13C and delta 18O values and interpret based on physiological theory
inputs: physiological interpretation rules (based on Barbour et al., Saurer & Siegwolf)
outputs: qualitative inference on drivers of Δ13C variation (gs vs Amax)

steps.description: The analysis of combined measurement of carbon an oxygen isotope ratio (Fig. 4) shows positive correlation between δ 13 C and δ 18 O in two periods of time (before mid-1980s and after mid-1990s).
steps.searchable_summary: qualitatively analyze temporal patterns in delta 13C and delta 18O correlation
inputs: correlation analysis results over different periods
outputs: description of temporal shifts in inferred physiological responses

methods.description: Procedure for collecting increment cores from Scots pine trees at selected sampling sites.

steps.description: Scots pines (P. sylvestris L.) is the main tree species in both forests and thus was selected for this research. ... the examined specimens were healthy, dominant in the stand, and had survived the adverse effects of industrial pollution. In order to avoid a different dendroecological reaction of juvenile wood, an attempt was made to select pine stands aged between 90 and 100 years (felling age of Scots pine).
steps.searchable_summary: select trees based on species, health, dominance, and age criteria

steps.description: In the summer of 2012, cores of the trees were collected at the breast height using a 5-mm-diameter increment borer. ... The α-cellulose samples were extracted from increment cores of ten representative trees.
steps.searchable_summary: collect increment cores from selected trees using increment borer

steps.description: The analyzed samples covered the time span from 1975 to 2012 AD.
steps.searchable_summary: verify that collected cores cover the required time period
outputs: validated sample set covering time span

methods.description: Procedure for extracting α-cellulose from annual tree rings and subsequently hydrolyzing it.

steps.description: The absolutely dated annual tree rings were ... manually separated as thin silvers
steps.searchable_summary: separate annual tree rings from increment cores

steps.description: pooled and homogenized.
steps.searchable_summary: pool and homogenize tree ring material for each year
inputs: separated annual tree ring silvers (from multiple trees for the same year)
outputs: pooled and homogenized tree ring material (per year)

steps.description: The α-cellulose samples were extracted by applying the procedures based on Green's method (1963) used in the Mass Spectrometry Laboratory of Silesian University of Technology (Pazdur et al. 2007(Pazdur et al. , 2013Sensuła et al. 2011a, b;Sensuła and Pazdur 2013a, b). This method includes the following steps: the removal of lignin, the processing of the holocellulose to α-cellulose, bleaching, neutralizing, and drying.
steps.searchable_summary: extract alpha-cellulose from wood material using chemical method
inputs: pooled and homogenized tree ring material
chemicals for Green's method (e.g., sodium chlorite, acetic acid, sodium hydroxide)

steps.description: The hydrolysis of α-cellulose by 72 % H 2 SO 4 was performed under the conditions described by Chambat et al. (1997) and was adopted by author in the Mass Spectrometry Laboratory of Silesian University of Technology in Poland (Sensuła et al. 2011b).
steps.searchable_summary: hydrolyze alpha-cellulose using sulfuric acid

steps.description: To ensure homogeneity, the chemical pretreatment was carried in an ultrasonic bath.
steps.searchable_summary: apply ultrasonic bath during chemical pretreatment steps

methods.description: Procedure for obtaining historical meteorological and industrial pollution emission data for the study area.

steps.description: The meteorological data were obtained thanks to the Polish Institute of Meteorology and Water Management (IMGW-PIB).
steps.searchable_summary: download or acquire meteorological data from IMGW-PIB

steps.description: The data of pollution emitted by the steelworks (sulfur dioxide and dusts) were obtained from the archives kindly provided by Huta Katowice.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire pollution emission data from Huta Katowice archives
inputs: data request/access to Huta Katowice archives

methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Meteorological and Pollution Data
methods.description: Statistical analysis performed on the acquired meteorological and pollution emission data.

steps.description: A significant correlation was detected between sulfur dioxide and industrial dust emission (r = 0.74, n = 35, p < 0.05).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Pearson correlation coefficient between two time series

steps.description: A significant correlation was detected between sulfur dioxide and industrial dust emission (r = 0.74, n = 35, p < 0.05).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sample size and p-value for correlation test

steps.description: For the statistical calculation, Statistica 10.0 software was applied.
